sections.content:
The red frame has a charcoal slot at [22,34] which is different from the gray slots. This is the middle slot of the red frame. Looking at the initial state, the palette had R, p, N, O, M, b, Y. The red frame is at the top and the green frame is at the bottom.
Maybe the goal is to sort colors by their frame:
- Red frame (3 slots): red-related colors
- Green frame (4 slots): green-related colors
But that doesn't make much sense with the 7 distinct colors.
Wait, let me reconsider. The charcoal slot at [22,34] is different. Maybe this is a "special" slot that needs to remain empty or has a different purpose.
Actually, let me look at the red frame more carefully. It has 4 slots: [22,22], [22,28], [22,34], [22,40]. But [22,34] is charcoal while the others are gray. The green frame has 4 slots: [36,22], [36,28], [36,34], [36,40], all gray.
Maybe the red frame has 3 usable slots (gray) + 1 special slot (charcoal), and the green frame has 4 usable slots. Total = 7 slots for 7 colors.
But the charcoal slot is at [22,34] which is the middle of the red frame. In the initial state, this was already charcoal.
Hmm, maybe the charcoal slot is not a slot but part of the frame structure? Let me look at the segmentation more carefully.
Actually, looking at the current frame output:
- color=c pixels=4 center=[22,34] - this is the charcoal slot (4 pixels = 2x2)
- color=g pixels=4 center=[22,22], [22,28], [22,40] - these are gray slots
So the charcoal slot IS a slot but with a different color. Maybe it needs a specific color?
Looking at the top row, the colors are: O, p, R, b, N, Y, M. The middle color (position 4) is b (blue). But the charcoal slot is in the red frame...
Wait, maybe the frames don't matter and the goal is just to sort the palette. But SPACE didn't work when the palette was sorted.
Let me try a different approach. Maybe the goal is to put colors in the frames (not the palette) to match the top row. Let me try putting all 7 colors in the frames in the correct order.
The frames have 7 slots total (4 in red frame + 4 in green frame, but one is charcoal). Let me try putting colors in the gray slots only (3 in red + 4 in green = 7 slots).
